$comment: D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK end-to-end witness: a DEEP-but-legal expression (200 nested parens around a literal, just under the 256 cap) compiles AND runs to exit 7 — exercising the WHOLE pipeline (parse -> analyze -> CST->HIR -> HIR->MIR -> codegen) at 200 nesting levels. The parser's maxExpressionDepth cap is 256 because (1) the speculative operand probe is O(N) via the LL(k) predictive prune (D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC) and (2) the deep-recursive frontend — the CU build (parse) AND the BUILD half (analyze + CST->HIR + HIR->MIR, each recursing one host-stack frame per nesting level) — runs on a 64 MiB worker stack (core/substrate/large_stack_call) instead of the host's ~1 MB main stack, which overflows at ~25 levels. 200 parens is far past the old crash point (~25) and old cap (16) and DEEP into HIR/MIR lowering (the stages an earlier draft left on the main stack); a regression that unwrapped any deep stage would crash (rc-127) here instead of exiting 7.
